# UC-22.22.27 · Customer Impact Assessment Coverage for Incidents

## Description

Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PSD2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes payments and sourcetypes pay:incident:milestone. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review. Indexes required: `index=payments`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pay:incident:milestone`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="cust"
| where milestone="impact_assessed"
| eval impact_missing=if(isnull(impacted_psu_estimate) OR impacted_psu_estimate<0,1,0)
| stats sum(impact_missing) as missing, dc(incident_id) as incidents by aspsp_id, severity
| eval uc_id="22.22.27"
| where missing>0
| sort - missing
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, severity, missing, incidents
```

Understanding this SPL

**Customer Impact Assessment Coverage for Incidents (PSD2)** — Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: pay:incident:milestone. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **inc_tag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where milestone="impact_assessed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **impact_missing** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **aspsp_id, severity**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where missing>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: aspsp_id, impact_missing, inc_tag, incidents, missing, severity, uc_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with payment switch or bank API logs, fraud case tool output, and the ServiceNow or GRC record for a known test transaction or drill window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)

## SPL

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="cust"
| where milestone="impact_assessed"
| eval impact_missing=if(isnull(impacted_psu_estimate) OR impacted_psu_estimate<0,1,0)
| stats sum(impact_missing) as missing, dc(incident_id) as incidents by aspsp_id, severity
| eval uc_id="22.22.27"
| where missing>0
| sort - missing
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, severity, missing, incidents
```

Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches). Key fields: `aspsp_id`, `impact_missing`, `inc_tag`, `incidents`, `missing`, `severity`.
